title: Cooktops
summary: Switching from a gas cooktop to an induction cooktop can significantly reduce home emissions and improve indoor air quality.
impact_text: This can cut stove-related carbon emissions by ~640 kg CO₂ every year.
steps: Check if your current cookware is induction-compatible (a magnet should stick to the bottom).
Compare induction cooktop options in your budget range.
Look for local utility rebates or state incentives before purchasing.
tip: If a full replacement is expensive, a portable induction burner is a lower-cost first step.

title: Light Bulbs
summary: Replacing incandescent bulbs with LEDs is one of the easiest high-impact upgrades you can make at home.
impact_text: Replacing 30 incandescent bulbs with 30 LED bulbs can reduce emissions by ~1,800 kg CO₂ every year.
steps: Count the bulbs in high-use areas (living room, kitchen, hallways).
Prioritize replacing incandescent or halogen bulbs first.
Choose LED bulbs with the brightness and color temperature you prefer.
Replace bulbs gradually room by room if needed to spread out cost.
tip: Look for ENERGY STAR LEDs and multi-pack discounts to lower upfront cost.
